title: Executive Order 10028—Defining Noncombatant Service and Noncombatant Training

flags.justification: A pure definitional order applying existing statutory terms; no new authority is created.
No ambiguity is created; this clarifies existing statutory terms.
Single purpose: defining two related terms for one statutory section.
The order's entire purpose is to define terms more precisely, and the definitions given are reasonably specific — this reduces rather than creates vagueness.
A pure definitional order creates no compliance regime of its own.
Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this is an evergreen definitional framework used repeatedly across Selective Service administration, not a temporary grant of authority.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
A pure definitional order with no compliance regime.
A narrow definitional clarification with no broader claimed authority.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
